title: Cheese scones
subtitle: Savoury scones with mature cheddar and mustard powder
excerpt: Cheese scones use the same rubbing-in method as sweet scones with grated cheddar and a little mustard powder added to the dough. They are higher-temperature and faster-baking than a cheese bread, with the same flaky crumb and crust as a plain scone.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136 — cheese scones appear as a savoury variant in the bread chapter alongside sweet scone recipes. The mature cheddar and mustard combination is the standard British form.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze baked and cooled. Warm from frozen at 180°C for 8 minutes.
recipe.batchNotes: Best on the day. Reheat briefly before serving.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cut and freeze unbaked on a tray. Bake from frozen at 220°C, adding 3–4 minutes.

glossaryTerms.definition: English mustard powder is used in small quantities in savoury cheese baking — cheese scones, gougères, cheese straws — not for its own flavour but to amplify the sharpness of the cheese. The mustard itself is not detectable in the finished bake; it functions as a flavour enhancer that makes the cheddar taste more intense.

content.text: The method is the same as plain scones.

amplifies the cheddar flavour without contributing mustard heat to the finished scone. Use the most mature cheddar you can find — mild cheddar produces a bland result. Reserve a small handful for sprinkling over the tops.

Heat the oven to 220°C (200°C fan). Sift {{self-raising-flour}}, {{baking-powder}}, {{english-mustard-powder}}, and {{salt-table}} into a large bowl. Rub in {{unsalted-butter}} until the mixture resembles breadcrumbs. Stir in most of the {{cheddar}}, reserving a handful. Add nearly all the {{whole-milk}} and mix quickly to a soft dough. Pat out to 3 cm thick on a floured surface and cut with a 6 cm cutter, pressing straight down without twisting.

Place on a lined tray. Brush the tops with beaten {{eggs}} and scatter the reserved {{cheddar}} over each one. Bake for 10–12 minutes until well risen and deep gold on top with the cheese topping bubbling. Eat warm.
